# omada_firewall_acls (Data Source)

Lists firewall ACL rules (across gateway, switch and EAP types) for a site — handy for discovering rule IDs to import.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Discover firewall ACL IDs and types (e.g. to write import blocks).
data "omada_firewall_acls" "all" {}

output "acl_ids" {
  value = { for a in data.omada_firewall_acls.all.acls : a.name => "${a.type}/${a.id}" }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Optional

- `site` (String) Site name to query (resolved to an ID). Ignored if `site_id` is set.
- `site_id` (String) Site ID to query. Provide this or `site`; if neither is set, the provider's default site is used.

### Read-Only

- `acls` (Attributes List) The firewall ACL rules defined on the site. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--acls))

<a id="nestedatt--acls"></a>
### Nested Schema for `acls`

Read-Only:

- `enabled` (Boolean) Whether the rule is enabled.
- `id` (String) The rule ID (use this to import `omada_firewall_acl`).
- `name` (String) The rule name.
- `policy` (Number) Policy: 0=deny, 1=permit.
- `type` (String) ACL type: `gateway`, `switch`, or `eap`.
